prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: In a cozy cove tucked beneath a waterfall of shimmering seaweed, lived a little octopus named Ollie. Ollie’s skin was the softest shade of twilight blue, and his eight gentle arms always seemed to be gently curling around himself, as if holding a secret. Though the ocean was full of bright fish who darted and spun, playful crabs who scuttled in circles, and giggly seahorses who danced in the currents, Ollie watched from a quiet coral nook. He adored them all—especially the way they laughed like bubbles and spun in swirling schools—but every time he thought about saying “Hello,” his heart would pitter-patter like a startled shrimp. Ollie hadn’t made a friend yet, but deep down, in the quietest part of his heart, he dreamed of sending out a single, brave message: *I’m here. I'd love to play.*
